muon_g2.formula: SM_WP2025 + (1/5)·(α_A3/π)³  [A3 measured alpha; N_channels as A5-averaged RT incidence mnemonic: (4·30−3·20)/12]
muon_g2.alpha_anchor: A3 measured low-energy alpha; bare GCT alpha remains O.19/O.5.

electron_g2.tier: Tier 2 kinematic-threshold mechanism + Tier 3 threshold exponent; alpha-input contingent
electron_g2.explanation: Kinematically forbidden (requires >101.2 MeV gap to depin N=11 resonance). Electron is ~198x too light to activate lattice loops.

tau_g2.form_factor_assumption: Assumes the same (1/5)(alpha/pi)^3 form-factor as the muon prediction; channel-count universality across leptons is Tier 3, candidate sub-problem of O.26
tau_g2.explanation: Tau (1776.93 MeV) is ~17.5x above the N=11 activation threshold (101.7 MeV), so the closed-phason-loop correction applies. Predicted Delta_a_tau equals the muon value under 5-channel A_5 form-factor universality (Tier 3 assumption). Current PDG bound (~1e-1) is 8 OOM weaker than the prediction; even the FCC-ee tau-g-2 program target sensitivity (~1e-6) is 3 OOM short. Prediction is real but currently unfalsifiable on operational grounds.

lamb_shift.note: Correction ∝ (l_Planck / a_Bohr)³ ≈ 2.9e-74. GCT makes no measurable prediction for the ordinary Lamb Shift.
